code: Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007

maintenance_and_welfare.Section 4: Maintenance of parents and senior citizens. A senior citizen or a parent who is unable to maintain himself from his own earnings or out of the property owned by him shall be entitled to make an application for maintenance against his children or relatives
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 5: Application for maintenance. Tribunal may suo motu or on application from senior citizen or parent or authorised person order children/relatives to pay monthly maintenance up to Rs 10,000 per month
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 7: Tribunal may order interim maintenance during pendency of proceedings
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 8: Maximum maintenance allowance. The maximum maintenance shall be Rs 10,000 per month per applicant from all children and relatives. Amendment 2019 proposed to increase this amount
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 9: Alteration in allowance. Tribunal may alter, modify or cancel the order on proof of change of circumstances
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 11: Tribunal may order the eviction of the children or relatives of the senior citizen from the premises of the senior citizen if they were given the property by way of gift or otherwise
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 12: Transfer of property by senior citizen. If any senior citizen who has transferred his property by way of gift or otherwise, is not being provided the basic amenities and basic physical needs, the transfer shall be deemed to have been made by fraud, coercion or undue influence and shall be declared void at the option of the transferor
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 21: Punishment for abandonment of senior citizen. Whoever having care of a senior citizen leaves such senior citizen in any place with the intention of wholly abandoning such senior citizen shall be punishable with imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs 5,000 or both
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 22: Exposure and neglect of senior citizen. Whoever having care of a senior citizen and being unable to maintain exposes or leaves in any place for the purpose of abandoning such senior citizen. Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs 5,000 or both
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 19: Establishment of old age homes by the State Government in every district for indigent senior citizens with capacity of at least 150 persons
maintenance_and_welfare.Section 20: Medical facilities for senior citizens. State Government shall ensure that medical facilities including beds in government hospitals for all senior citizens are made available
